tipRules.tip: Johnston: Use a tungsten drop shot weight with a 6th Sense Party Minnow on a BKK drop-shot hook for St. Lawrence smallmouth across sand flats, rockpiles, weed edges, and deep high spots from shallow to 35-40 feet.
Johnston: Use a Ned rig as a secondary presentation when targeting St. Lawrence smallmouth alongside a drop shot.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, 24 pounds really isn't a good day and anything over 25 is what you need to win. It's a special place.
Johnston: Catch St. Lawrence smallmouth across a wide variety of habitats in a single day: shallow sand flats, rockpiles, weed edges, and deep high spots, rocks, and deeper weed edges in 35-40 feet.
Johnston: Hit as many spots as possible until you run into the bigger fish. Quality bites often come late in the day after working through many locations.
Johnston: At the St. Lawrence, bigger smallmouth (5.5 to 6.75 lbs) often come from shallow water rather than deep.
